channel.title: Logitech — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Logitech (logitech).

item.title: CVE-2024-8258 · 2 LOW — Insecure Electron Fuses in Logitech Options Plus Allowing Arbitrary Code Execution on macOS
item.description: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection') in Electron Fuses in Logitech Options Plus version 1.60.496306 on macOS allows attackers to execute arbitrary code via insecure Electron Fuses configuration.

item.title: CVE-2024-8011 · 2 LOW
item.description: Logitech Options+ on MacOS prior 1.72 allows a local attacker to inject dynamic library within Options+ runtime and abuse permissions granted by the user to Options+ such as Camera.

item.title: CVE-2024-4031 · 4.4 MEDIUM — MEVO WEBCAM APP Windows Unquoted Service Path Vulnerability
item.description: Unquoted Search Path or Element vulnerability in Logitech MEVO WEBCAM APP on Windows allows Local Execution of Code.

item.title: CVE-2024-2537 · 4.4 MEDIUM — Electron Code Injection in Logi Tune macOS Application
item.description: Improper Control of Dynamically-Managed Code Resources vulnerability in Logitech Logi Tune on MacOS allows Local Code Inclusion.

item.title: CVE-2022-0916 · 8.4 HIGH — Broken authentication on Logitech Options due to misvalidation of Oauth state parameter
item.description: An issue was discovered in Logitech Options. The OAuth 2.0 state parameter was not properly validated. This leaves applications vulnerable to CSRF attacks during authentication and authorization operations.

item.title: CVE-2022-0915 · 6 MEDIUM — Logitech Sync desktop application prior to 2.4.574 - TOCTOU during installation leads to privelege escalation
item.description: There is a Time-of-check Time-of-use (TOCTOU) Race Condition Vulnerability in Logitech Sync for Windows prior to 2.4.574. Successful exploitation of these vulnerabilities may escalate the permission to the system user.
